title: SimConfig
description: Declarative configuration for a per-industry simulation, consumed by the polis/sim/ substrate kernel. One SimConfig per industry-sim per world. Authoring contract for any new industry sim (football match, basketball game, court case, hostile takeover, election, steel production, boxing, music recording session). The kernel is industry-agnostic; this schema captures everything an industry needs to declare to plug into it. Per DECISION-SIM-ENGINE.md (2026-05-20). Pydantic v2 source in polis/sim/config.py is the canonical interim until codegen flips JSON Schema → Pydantic per V0-PLAN §5.

industry_id.description: Slug identifier matching the parent industry config (e.g. 'football-modern-earth', 'basketball-toy'). Foreign key to industry.schema.json instance.

driver.description:
Time-stepping pattern.
- 'event': event-driven scheduler; next event scheduled from current state. Match-shaped (football, basketball).
- 'tick': fixed-cadence; every N seconds, all active actors process. Production-shaped (steel, manufacturing).
- 'turn': turn-based; one actor acts per turn, round-robin. Legal-shaped (court case, negotiation).

end_condition.description: When the sim stops. Exactly one set of conditional fields is honored per type.

type.description:
- 'duration': end at sim_time >= sim_seconds.
- 'target_state': end when state matches a predicate.
- 'event_count': end after N events of a specific type (e.g., 12 rounds of boxing).

sim_seconds.description: Required when type='duration'. Sim-time seconds since start.

target_state.description: Required when type='target_state'. Sub-tree of sim state to match. Industry-defined.

event_type.description: Required when type='event_count'. The action_type to count toward the end threshold.

count.description: Required when type='event_count'. Number of matching events that triggers end.

roles.description: Actor roles this sim expects. Validated at sim setup against the role counts. Industry-defined vocabulary (goalkeeper, judge, factory_foreman, voter).

schema_ref.description: Which substrate schema actors in this role validate against. Almost always character.schema.json; could be institution.schema.json for institutional actors (a club, a regulator).

min_count.description: Minimum number of actors required in this role for the sim to start.

max_count.description: Maximum number of actors allowed in this role. Null means unbounded.

initial_state.description: Starting state of the sim. Industry-specific shape — kernel does not introspect. Football: {score: {home: 0, away: 0}, possession: 'home'}. Court case: {phase: 'opening', objections: 0}.

events.description: Event vocabulary for this sim. Each event has an industry-defined action_type, a Python handler that mutates state + schedules follow-ups, and optionally a decision_point flag that gates an LLM hook call.

id.description: Industry-defined verb. Examples: 'pass', 'shot', 'objection', 'produce', 'vote_cast', 'punch_thrown'. The kernel emits Events with this as action_type.

handler.description: Dotted Python path to the handler. Signature: 'async def handler(sim: Sim, ctx: Action | None) -> None'. Loaded via importlib at sim startup; missing modules fail fast.

decision_point.description: If true, kernel calls LLMHook.decide() before invoking the handler; the returned Action is passed as ctx. Use for moments where a character chooses (shoot vs pass, object vs concede, fire vs hire).

decision_type.description: Required if decision_point=true. Passed to the LLM hook in DecisionContext.decision_type. Examples: 'shot_choice', 'objection_call', 'transfer_offer_response'.

legal_actions.description: Industry-defined action vocabulary the LLM hook is allowed to return. Empty list means the handler accepts any action_type string. Stub hooks pick from this list deterministically.

output_schema_ref.description: Schema that FinalState.outcome conforms to after the sim ends. Default points at the canonical sim_result schema (not yet shipped — pending follow-up). Industry-specific output shapes can override.

metadata.description: Free-form: version, author, calibration target dataset reference, source attribution, last_calibrated_at timestamp, notes.

version.description: Semver of this sim config — bumped on rule changes that affect output distributions.

purpose.description: Editorial — what this sim is for. Especially important for fixture configs (e.g. 'kernel-validation only, not production').

calibration_target_ref.description: Path to the per-industry calibration target distributions (e.g. 'polis/configs/industries/football-modern-earth/sim/calibration/epl.yaml').

not_production.description: Marks fixture / test configs that must never be loaded in alpha or prod. Engine checks this at startup.

Note_pending_artifacts.description: Pending substrate artifacts referenced by this schema (per polis/sim/README.md 'Pending'): polis/schemas/sim_result.schema.json (FinalState.outcome shape) and the codegen pipeline (V0-PLAN §5) that produces Pydantic + SQLAlchemy + TypeScript from this JSON Schema. Until codegen lands, polis/sim/config.py is the canonical Pydantic source and must be kept in sync with this schema by hand.
